zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont於大約1138年出生在法國曼恩時，他的父親Roscelin de Beaumont45歲，母親Constance fitzRoy20歲。他於1160年之前與Lucie de l'Aigle在法國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他在法國被列為vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count )。他去世於1194年在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特波奧蒙吉素-薩特享年57歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特米雷--哈尼阿巴易 達艾迪瓦爾--哈尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont于大约1138年出生在法国曼恩时，他的父亲Roscelin de Beaumont45岁，母亲Constance fitzRoy20岁。他于1160年之前与Lucie de l'Aigle在法国结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他在法国被列为vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count )。他去世于1194年在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特波奧蒙吉素-薩特享年57岁，埋葬在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特米雷--哈尼阿巴易 達艾迪瓦爾--哈尼。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont was born about 1138, in Maine, France, his father, Roscelin de Beaumont, was 45 and his mother, Constance fitzRoy, was 20. He married Lucie de l'Aigle before 1160, in France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. His occupation is listed as vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) in France. He died in 1194, in Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, at the age of 57, and was buried in Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont est né environ 1138, à Maine, France, son père, Roscelin de Beaumont, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Constance fitzRoy, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Lucie de l'Aigle avant 1160, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) à France. Il est décédé en 1194, à Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont um 1138 in Maine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roscelin de Beaumont 45 und seine Mutter Constance fitzRoy 20. Er hat vor 1160 in Frankreich Lucie de l'Aigle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) in Frankreich aufgeführt. Er starb 1194 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich und wurde in Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont è nato il circa 1138 nel Maine, Francia, il suo padre, Roscelin de Beaumont, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Constance fitzRoy, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Lucie de l'Aigle prima del 1160, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) nel Francia. Morì nel 1194, nel Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia.

ja.Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-C: フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスケミールe-エン-チャーニーアッバイ ド'eティヴァル-エン-チャーニー

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumontが約1138年にフランスメインで生まれた時，父Roscelin de Beaumontは45歳，母Constance fitzRoyは20歳であった。1160年以前にフランスでLucie de l'Aigleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。フランスでの職業はvicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count )と記録されている。彼は1194年にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスビーウモント-スル-サルスで57歳で死去し，フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスケミールe-エン-チャーニーアッバイ ド'eティヴァル-エン-チャーニーにて埋葬された。

ko.Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, 72170, France: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 보몽-수-사르테
ko.Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 보몽-수-사르테
ko.Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-C: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 체미e--처니 애브베이 드'e티발--처니
ko.Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Mamers, Sarthe, Pay: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 맘르스 보몽-수-사르테
ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont(이)가 대략 1138년 프랑스 맹에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roscelin de Beaumont(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance fitzRoy(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1160년 이전 프랑스에서 Lucie de l'Aigle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 프랑스에서 vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count )이었다. 그는 1194년 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 보몽-수-사르테에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 체미e--처니 애브베이 드'e티발--처니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont nasceu aproximadamente 1138, em Maine, França, seu pai, Roscelin de Beaumont, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Constance fitzRoy, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucie de l'Aigle antes 1160, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) em França. Ele faleceu em 1194, em Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Mamers, Sarthe, Pay: бомонт-сур-сарт, мамирс, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont родился около 1138 г. в местности меин, Франция, его отцу по имени Roscelin de Beaumont было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance fitzRoy, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucie de l'Aigle перед 1160 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери.  Он работал по профессии vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) в местности Франция. Он умер в 1194 г. в местности бомонт-сур-сарт, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности эббеи д'eтивал-ен-чарни, чемирe-ен-чарни, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Beaumont Seigneur of Beaumont nació aproximadamente 1138, en Maine, Francia, su papá, Roscelin de Beaumont, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Constance fitzRoy, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Lucie de l'Aigle antes de 1160, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era vicomte  (a nobleman next below an earl or count ) en Francia. Murió en 1194, en Beaumont-sur-Sarthe, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Abbaye d'Étival-en-Charnie, Chemiré-en-Charnie, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia.
